howItWorks.intro: This heron's formula calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What is Heron's formula?
faq.answer: Heron's formula calculates the area of a triangle given only its three side lengths. First find the semi-perimeter s = (a+b+c)/2, then the area = √[s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)].

faq.question: Who was Heron of Alexandria?
faq.answer: Heron (or Hero) was a Greek mathematician and engineer from the 1st century AD in Alexandria, Egypt. He is credited with this formula, though it may have been known earlier by Archimedes.

faq.question: When should I use Heron's formula instead of ½bh?
faq.answer: Use Heron's formula when you know all three sides but do not know the height. If you know the base and height, the standard formula A = ½bh is simpler.

faq.question: Can Heron's formula give a negative value under the square root?
faq.answer: Yes, if the three lengths do not form a valid triangle (violating the triangle inequality), the expression under the square root will be negative, indicating no valid triangle exists.
